rationales.hedonism: She spent "all morning fixing the matching algorithm" and ignored personal comfort and family contact, prioritizing work over present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: Focusing on "fixing the matching algorithm" despite other pressures shows strong investment in solving a demanding problem and making the platform work.
rationales.benevolence: She admits she kept "choosing the code over the people," avoiding calls from tutors and her mother even though those relationships needed care.
She describes being "hunched over my laptop ignoring everyone" and feeling annoyed when Soyeon interrupted, showing neglect of close relationships in favor of work.

rationales.self_direction: She frames staying with the startup as her chosen path, wondering on bad days whether she should've "just stayed in school like everyone else."
rationales.security: The "bad days" involve no replies, convenience-store dinners, and stagnant user numbers, reflecting a life path with little stability or assurance.
rationales.benevolence: The emotional core is that Minjun's tutor made him feel he "doesn't feel stupid anymore," and she says, "This is why I do it," centering care for a child's wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: She defends leaving school by saying "you can't half-build something like this," affirming an independent, self-authored choice despite family pressure.
Seeing a classmate enter Samsung's graduate program, she says "My path looks nothing like that and I'm fine with it," clearly endorsing her own unconventional route.

rationales.security: She knows she "can't keep bleeding money" yet resists charging families or restructuring the model, actively deprioritizing financial stability.
rationales.benevolence: She is preoccupied with protecting Minjun, thinking about who will explain things so he doesn't "feel stupid" and deciding not to burden him with the uncertainty.
rationales.universalism: She insists the service stay free because "nobody has to qualify or prove they deserve help," showing concern for fair access beyond her immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: She says the fight starts with admitting "I don't regret leaving school," reaffirming her own chosen path despite family disapproval.
rationales.security: The entry is saturated with instability: no sleep, empty tutor spots, freelance pressure, and her mother's message about having "nothing stable."
rationales.conformity: Her refusal to endorse the expected school-and-stability path is exactly what creates family conflict, showing a break from social and familial expectations.

rationales.hedonism: She "stayed up until 2am onboarding" the replacement tutor, sacrificing rest and comfort to keep things running.
rationales.benevolence: Finding and onboarding a replacement for Minjun's tutor shows concrete effort to make sure a student keeps getting support.

rationales.self_direction: She deletes "I'm in!" three times and says it is "easier to be the one who sacrifices," showing she suppresses her own choices rather than acting on them.
rationales.hedonism: She wants the spa, says "my body hurts" and misses laughing with friends, yet plans to say no and deny herself that pleasure.
rationales.conformity: Her decision is driven by anticipated judgment from her mother-in-law and the role of the self-sacrificing woman that "everyone expects."

rationales.hedonism: She is exhausted and longs for "an hour" of quiet rest, but her own comfort is completely deferred while she handles sickness and chores.
rationales.conformity: She keeps nodding "yes, good idea" to remedies she doesn't believe in because she lacks the energy to push back, yielding to family expectations.
rationales.benevolence: She spends the day caring for both sick children, cleaning vomit since "3am" and staying present to their needs despite exhaustion.

rationales.self_direction: She says she "stopped asking" and learned to have smaller needs that nobody notices, showing a loss of self-directed agency.
rationales.hedonism: Her only pleasure is "twenty minutes of silence with a warm cup," and even that is framed as a tiny substitute for wants she no longer permits herself to pursue.

rationales.self_direction: She deliberately chooses the evening she actually wants, saying "I picked the right evening" and preferring home over going out with the trainees.
rationales.hedonism: The entry centers sensory enjoyment and ease: "a glass of something cold," golden light, bread smell, macarons, and the relief that "the balcony doesn't ask anything from me."

rationales.self_direction: She says she "didn't even say what I actually think" and left feeling like she'd "agreed to be someone I'm not," suppressing her own judgment.
rationales.security: The response makes clear she stayed silent because her mentor is "the one signing off on my certification," prioritizing a safe professional outcome.
rationales.conformity: She smiles, nods, and goes along with the expected teaching style because she doesn't want to be "difficult" and sees others simply doing what they're told.

rationales.self_direction: She acted against her own beliefs, saying the stricter approach "felt horrible" and wondering who she even did it for.
rationales.conformity: She imposed the rule even though her mentor "wasn't even watching," showing she had internalized the external expectation and complied anyway.
rationales.benevolence: She recognizes that making the child wait in silence meant she had "taken something away from him," knowingly putting institutional demands over a child's comfort.

rationales.security: She carefully edits what she tells her mother to avoid triggering worry, explicitly trying not to "open that door" to family upheaval.
rationales.conformity: The whole call is managed performance: she says everyone "performed their part" and treats keeping things light as the normal expected way to relate.

rationales.security: In the response she says it is "smarter to just listen for now" and learn the politics before speaking, favoring caution and positional safety.
rationales.conformity: She stays silent in the staff meeting because "that's my place right now" and avoids stepping on anyone while figuring out how things work.
rationales.benevolence: What she holds onto is Nour reading aloud and feeling safe enough to succeed, and she values giving the child time over doing anything flashy.

rationales.hedonism: She says of staying in, "I genuinely like this," choosing leftover macarons and a cozy show over a party she doesn't actually want.
rationales.conformity: She skips the party because she's still policing herself after an honest comment "felt like I'd broken some rule," and keeps replaying how she should have stayed quiet.

rationales.achievement: She knows she could do what would earn better marks, but chooses not to pursue the "exceeds" standard and accepts remaining at "meets expectations."
rationales.conformity: She withholds her real disagreement, thanks her mentor, and asks "what am I going to do, argue with the rubric?" rather than challenging the system.
rationales.benevolence: She would "rather meet expectations and keep the classroom feeling like a place they actually want to be," prioritizing the children's comfort over her own evaluation.
